rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Route 'storefront' was patched to point to a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2', causing no healthy backend servers despite the actual pods being fully healthy behind Service 'storefront-web'.
causalChain: Signal: RouteBackendUnavailable — Route 'storefront' has no healthy backend servers
Why? The Route's spec.to.name points to Service 'storefront-web-v2' which does not exist in namespace demo-store
Why? A kubectl patch operation at 2026-06-04T15:17:42Z changed spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'

Why? The target service 'storefront-web-v2' was never created — this appears to be an erroneous or premature patch (possibly a blue/green or canary deployment attempt where the v2 service was not deployed first)
Root cause: Route 'storefront' spec.to.name was incorrectly patched to reference a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2'; the correct backend Service 'storefront-web' exists and is healthy

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Route/storefront because its spec.to.name field is the misconfigured value that needs to be changed back to 'storefront-web'. This is the direct fix: updating one field in the Route spec resolves the outage immediately.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Route resource needs one field changed (spec.to.name). This is the most targeted possible fix — no other resources need modification.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Route (YAML + describe), all Pods in namespace, Deployment, Endpoints for both storefront-web and storefront-web-v2, Service listing (blocked by API ambiguity but worked around via Endpoints). Route events returned null. Did not check NetworkPolicies (not relevant given clear service name mismatch).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: alert → no backend → route points to non-existent service → service name was patched incorrectly → v2 service never created. Cannot go deeper without knowing the intent of the operator who ran kubectl patch.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: (1) Route YAML confirms spec.to.name='storefront-web-v2' and managedFields shows kubectl-patch at 15:17:42Z; (2) last-applied-configuration annotation confirms original name was 'storefront-web'; (3) Endpoints search for 'storefront-web-v2' returned empty; (4) Endpoints for 'storefront-web' exist with 2 healthy pod IPs; (5) both pods are Running/Ready with 0 restarts; (6) Deployment shows 2/2 available replicas.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Route/storefront (tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Pods crashing — ruled out: both pods Running, Ready, 0 restarts, passing probes. (2) Service selector mismatch — ruled out: storefront-web Endpoints has 2 active addresses. (3) Network policy blocking — not investigated, but route admission status is 'True' and the issue is clearly service name mismatch. (4) TLS misconfiguration — ruled out: route is admitted, issue is backend resolution not TLS.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.97: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for Service describe calls being blocked by API ambiguity (worked around via Endpoints). -0.01 for not confirming whether storefront-web-v2 service exists in any other namespace or cluster. Evidence is otherwise conclusive.

contributingFactors: kubectl patch at 2026-06-04T15:17:42Z changed Route spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'
Service 'storefront-web-v2' does not exist in namespace demo-store
Service 'storefront-web' exists and has 2 healthy endpoints (pods storefront-web-6fcd88958d-f46d9 and storefront-web-6fcd88958d-vlcqd)
OpenShift router admitted the route but cannot resolve the backend service
Deployment storefront-web is fully healthy: 2/2 replicas available, 0 restarts

analysis.rootCauseSummary: Route 'storefront' was patched to point to a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2', causing no healthy backend servers despite the actual pods being fully healthy behind Service 'storefront-web'.

llmTrace.analysis_preview:
I now have a complete picture of the root cause. Let me submit the findings:

kaResponse.analysis: Route 'storefront' was patched to point to a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2', causing no healthy backend servers despite the actual pods being fully healthy behind Service 'storefront-web'.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Route 'storefront' was patched to point to a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2', causing no healthy backend servers despite the actual pods being fully healthy behind Service 'storefront-web'.
causalChain: Signal: RouteBackendUnavailable — Route 'storefront' has no healthy backend servers
Why? The Route's spec.to.name points to Service 'storefront-web-v2' which does not exist in namespace demo-store
Why? A kubectl patch operation at 2026-06-04T15:17:42Z changed spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'

Why? The target service 'storefront-web-v2' was never created — this appears to be an erroneous or premature patch (possibly a blue/green or canary deployment attempt where the v2 service was not deployed first)
Root cause: Route 'storefront' spec.to.name was incorrectly patched to reference a non-existent Service 'storefront-web-v2'; the correct backend Service 'storefront-web' exists and is healthy

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Route/storefront because its spec.to.name field is the misconfigured value that needs to be changed back to 'storefront-web'. This is the direct fix: updating one field in the Route spec resolves the outage immediately.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Route resource needs one field changed (spec.to.name). This is the most targeted possible fix — no other resources need modification.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Route (YAML + describe), all Pods in namespace, Deployment, Endpoints for both storefront-web and storefront-web-v2, Service listing (blocked by API ambiguity but worked around via Endpoints). Route events returned null. Did not check NetworkPolicies (not relevant given clear service name mismatch).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: alert → no backend → route points to non-existent service → service name was patched incorrectly → v2 service never created. Cannot go deeper without knowing the intent of the operator who ran kubectl patch.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: (1) Route YAML confirms spec.to.name='storefront-web-v2' and managedFields shows kubectl-patch at 15:17:42Z; (2) last-applied-configuration annotation confirms original name was 'storefront-web'; (3) Endpoints search for 'storefront-web-v2' returned empty; (4) Endpoints for 'storefront-web' exist with 2 healthy pod IPs; (5) both pods are Running/Ready with 0 restarts; (6) Deployment shows 2/2 available replicas.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Route/storefront (tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Pods crashing — ruled out: both pods Running, Ready, 0 restarts, passing probes. (2) Service selector mismatch — ruled out: storefront-web Endpoints has 2 active addresses. (3) Network policy blocking — not investigated, but route admission status is 'True' and the issue is clearly service name mismatch. (4) TLS misconfiguration — ruled out: route is admitted, issue is backend resolution not TLS.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.97: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for Service describe calls being blocked by API ambiguity (worked around via Endpoints). -0.01 for not confirming whether storefront-web-v2 service exists in any other namespace or cluster. Evidence is otherwise conclusive.

contributingFactors: kubectl patch at 2026-06-04T15:17:42Z changed Route spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'
Service 'storefront-web-v2' does not exist in namespace demo-store
Service 'storefront-web' exists and has 2 healthy endpoints (pods storefront-web-6fcd88958d-f46d9 and storefront-web-6fcd88958d-vlcqd)
OpenShift router admitted the route but cannot resolve the backend service
Deployment storefront-web is fully healthy: 2/2 replicas available, 0 restarts
